title: quercus calliprinos var. brachybalanos
intro: Quercus calliprinos var. brachybalanos, also known as the Cypriot or Cyprus oak, is a variety of evergreen tree native to the island of Cyprus in the eastern Mediterranean. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health, due to its high levels of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Quercus calliprinos var. brachybalanos include reduced inflammation, antioxidant effects, and the potential to mitigate the risk of certain diseases such as heart disease and cancer. Quercus calliprinos var. brachybalanos is traditionally used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, to promote overall well-being and support the immune system.
